commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=886aa3839d7012b32460032dd0ee075916ffe962)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a `zhipu` provider exception to `isSilentOverflowProneModel` and updates focused agent-settings tests/comments for zhipu GLM models.

PR surface: Source +8, Tests +16. Total +24 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR-introduced regression at source level: with `provider: "zhipu"` plus `baseUrl: "https://api.z.ai/..."` or `modelId: "z-ai/glm-5.1"`, the new early return runs before the existing z.ai checks. The original zhipu/GLM-5.2 wedge is not proven by real behavior evidence in the PR.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor code changes plus external real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not queue an automated repair while the required proof is missing.

target.title: fix(agent-settings): exclude zhipu provider from silentOverflowProneModel detection
target.body:
## Problem

Dennis (zhipu/GLM-5.2) users experience transcript wedging because isSilentOverflowProneModel incorrectly flags zhipu models as silent-overflow-prone via the glm- prefix match. This disables SDK auto-compaction for those models.

## Root Cause

isSilentOverflowProneModel uses model id prefix matching (glm-) without considering the provider. Zhipu GLM models use a different overflow strategy than the pattern was designed to catch.

## Fix

Add early exit when provider === "zhipu" before checking model id patterns. Updated function JSDoc to document the zhipu exclusion.

## Tests

- Regression tests for zhipu provider with:
  - bare glm- model id
  - glm-4 model id
  - z-ai/ model id prefix
- Existing test "regardless of provider" renamed to acknowledge zhipu exception

## Impact

Only affects isSilentOverflowProneModel for the zhipu provider. No other providers are impacted by this change.
